title: Rigetti Drops 108 Qubits: The Quantum Roadmap Wall Street Is Sleeping On
subtitle: $569M cash, zero debt, three government deals, and a clear path to 1,000+ qubits — yet $RGTI is still down YTD
summary: Rigetti's 108-qubit Cepheus system is live on AWS, Azure, and qBraid. With $569M in cash, three government deals, and a roadmap targeting quantum advantage by 2029, $RGTI looks underappreciated.

image.caption: Professional macro photograph. Extreme close-up of a quantum processor chip with superconducting circuits, gold-plated traces on sapphire substrate, intricate wiring patterns. Dramatic blue/purple lighting.

bodyHtml:
One hundred and eight superconducting qubits just went generally available — and the market barely blinked. Rigetti Computing ($RGTI) launched Cepheus-1-108Q in April 2026, now live across Amazon Braket, Microsoft Azure, and qBraid. That same month, the U.S. Department of Commerce signed a $100 million CHIPS Act letter of intent. An $8.4 million order from India's C-DAC for an on-premises 108-qubit system followed. The UK committed another $100 million for a 1,000+ qubit system within 3-4 years. The receipts are stacking up — and $RGTI is still down 9.49% YTD at $21.36 with a $7.1 billion market cap.

The Roadmap Is Moving Faster Than the Narrative

Rigetti's trajectory is laid out with unusual specificity: 150+ qubits by end of 2026, scaling to 1,000+ qubits by end of 2027, with quantum advantage targeted around 2029. The 108-qubit system runs on a 12-chiplet modular architecture — the industry's largest — with 2-qubit gate fidelity at 99.1% in 60 nanoseconds. A prototype hit 99.9% fidelity at just 28ns, pushing error rates where useful computation becomes real. Revenue confirms the acceleration: Q1 2026 printed $4.4 million, up 198.9% year-over-year from $7.1 million for all of FY2025. One quarter already beat half of last year's entire haul.

▶ Rigetti CEO on Quantum Advantage Timeline
Rigetti Computing CEO: We are about 3-4 years away from demonstrating quantum advantage — CNBC Television

The Balance Sheet Is the Real Moat

Where cash burn is the norm and dilution is a quarterly ritual, Rigetti's balance sheet is a fortress: $569 million in cash with zero debt. The $100 million CHIPS Act LOI further de-risks the capital picture. While competitors race to raise at dilutive terms, Rigetti is fully-funded through its most critical scaling years. A $7.1 billion market cap against half a billion in cash puts enterprise value at roughly $6.5 billion — not cheap, but defensible for a company targeting a market projected to exceed $100 billion by the mid-2030s.

The Risk/Reward Calculus

Let's not sugarcoat it: 18.77% of the float is short. Bears aren't irrational — quantum computing remains pre-revenue. $4.4 million quarterly doesn't move the needle on a $7 billion valuation. Nobody's proven this works at scale yet. A stumble on the 150-qubit milestone or a fidelity regression would gut the stock. But the short thesis has to contend with stacked catalysts: GA product on three cloud platforms, government contracts with real dollars, a modular architecture that scales, and fidelities trending right. Twelve analysts carry a consensus Buy with a mean target of $29.24 — roughly 37% upside from $21.36.

The 108-qubit milestone is impressive, but the 12-chiplet modular architecture is the real story — it's the platform that gets Rigetti to 1,000+ qubits without reinventing the wheel.

Bottom Line

$RGTI isn't for the faint of heart. Eighteen percent short interest means every positive catalyst is a squeeze trigger and every miss is carnage. But the setup is increasingly asymmetric: a half-billion-dollar cash fortress, zero debt, live hardware on three cloud platforms, and a government-backed roadmap — trading at a YTD loss while AI compute mints trillion-dollar valuations. If quantum follows AI's trajectory from curiosity to critical infrastructure, $21.36 will look like a gift. The 2029 quantum advantage target is three years out. The market prices things six months at a time. That gap is the opportunity.

— The SignalThis article is for informational purposes only and does not constitute financial advice. The Signal may hold positions in securities mentioned.
